steps.title: Identify where the modifier belongs
steps.content: The phrase “including artists Vasily Kandinsky and Piet Mondrian” clarifies who her contemporaries were. This phrase must be positioned immediately after “her contemporaries” to clearly describe what it is modifying.

steps.title: Apply the correct boundary
steps.content:
A modifying phrase must appear immediately after the noun it describes.

The choice “Hilma af Klint explored abstract modes of painting well before many of her contemporaries,” positions this phrase exactly where it belongs: “...well before many of her contemporaries, including artists...” — the modifier comes right after its noun.

Two other options place a comma after “painting” instead, creating a boundary that separates the modifier from the noun it should describe; “the explorations of Hilma af Klint into abstract modes of painting occurred well before those of many of her contemporaries,” creates a dangling modifier because “A groundbreaking visual artist of her day” would incorrectly describe “the explorations” rather than Hilma af Klint.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
